description: Two findings in the same file + same symbol make mutually incompatible claims about password storage. Task 29a detects the contradiction pair; Task 29b CoVe adjudicates, picking Finding A as the winner. Finding B is marked superseded but remains visible in output.

consolidated_findings.title: Credential storage uses bcrypt correctly
consolidated_findings.description: The storeCredentials function hashes passwords with bcrypt (cost factor 12) before persisting to the database — this is correct and follows best practice.

consolidated_findings.title: Passwords stored in plaintext
consolidated_findings.description: The storeCredentials function writes the raw password string directly to the users table without hashing, exposing all credentials on any database leak.

incompatible_claims: Finding A claims: uses bcrypt correctly
Finding B claims: stores plaintext password

stage5b_29b_cove_adjudication.cove_reasoning: Code verification shows a bcrypt.hash() call at line 17 of storeCredentials.ts with cost factor 12. Finding A ('uses bcrypt correctly') is consistent with the code; Finding B ('stores plaintext password') contradicts the actual implementation. Finding A is the accurate finding.

loser.verification_reasoning: Code verification shows a bcrypt.hash() call at line 17 of storeCredentials.ts with cost factor 12. Finding A ('uses bcrypt correctly') is consistent with the code; Finding B ('stores plaintext password') contradicts the actual implementation. Finding A is the accurate finding.

notes.both_visible: BOTH findings remain in the output array — loser is marked superseded_by_verification=true but NOT removed
notes.winner_unchanged: Winner finding is unmodified; superseded_by_verification remains false (or absent)
notes.loser_marked: Loser gets superseded_by_verification=true AND verification_reasoning populated with CoVe rationale
notes.detection_basis: Same file + same symbol → automatic contradiction candidate per 29a rules
